zh.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clevedon於大約1290年出生在英國戴文默頓。她於大約1309年與Sir Richard I de Merton在英國英格蘭戴文默頓結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和3個女兒的父母。她去世於1367年在英國戴文格亞特 托林頓享年78歲。

nameMeanings.name: Clevedon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from Cleverdon in Devon.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Deborah
meanings: Biblical name (meaning ‘bee’ in Hebrew), borne by the nurse of Rebecca (Genesis 35:8) and by a woman judge and prophet (Judges 4–5) who led the Israelites to victory over the Canaanites. It has always been popular as a Jewish name. It was in use among Christians by the mid 16th century and was taken up by the Puritans in the 17th century, in part because the bee was a symbol of industriousness. Since then it has enjoyed enormous popularity, peaking in the 1960s. Among other famous bearers is the actress  Deborah Kerr  (  1921–2007 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clevedon was born about 1290, in Merton, Devon, England. She married Sir Richard I de Merton about 1309, in Merton, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 3 daughters. She died in 1367, in Great Torrington, Devon, England, at the age of 78.

Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 3 filles. Elle est décédée en 1367, à Great Torrington, Devon, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clevedonは約1290年にイギリスデボンマートンで生まれた。約1309年にイギリスイギリスデボンマートンでSir Richard I de Mertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼女は1367年にイギリスデボングレアト トリントンで78歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clevedon(은)는 대략 1290년 영국 데폰 머턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1309년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 머턴에서 Sir Richard I de Merton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1367년 영국 데폰 그리트 토링턴에서 78세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret de Clevedon родилась около 1290 г. в местности миртон, девон, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Richard I de Merton около 1309 г. в местности миртон, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 3 дочери. Она умерла в 1367 г. в местности грит торрингтон, девон, Англия в возрасте 78 лет.
